title: Seeker AS-M12 message droid

The '''Seeker AS-M12''' was a [[Courier droid/Legends|messenger droid]] variant manufactured by [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd Industries]].

==Description==
[[File:AS-M12Seeker2-FF61.png|thumb|left|200px|A Seeker AS-M12 message droid]]
The Seeker AS-M12 was spherical in shape and was smaller than a fully grown [[Human/Legends|Human]]. It was also equipped with [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlifts]] for hovering over the ground. Such [[Droid/Legends|droids]] were programmed to locate their targets and deliver their messages quickly. Typically they were transported across [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] via [[Hyperdrive pod/Legends|hyperdrive pods]], a droid-controlled [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]-equipped pod, which only made one-way trips.

It also had sensitive visual and audio sensors and had a retractable [[Manipulator arm/Legends|manipulator arm]] used to open doors and operate [[Turbolift/Legends|turbolifts]]. It could also plug into public directories with a [[I/O jack|computer input jack]].

[[File:AS-M12_message_droid-WEG.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An AS-M12 with its grasper arm extended.]]
When its target has been located, the individual is required to provide a verbal passcode before the droid can replay its message. Visual data is projected by a [[Holoprojector/Legends|holoprojector]] while audio data is filtered by its [[Vocabulator/Legends|vocabulator]]. Such droids were costly and each [[unit]] cost 24,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].

Occasionally such droids were used as covert [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]], carrying explosives inside their holds. This attempt at ingenuity was known to backfire on the attacker, due to the fact that the droid kept a hard-wired log of from where it was deployed—enabling the droid to find its way back home.

==Appearances==
*[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' novel]] {{1st|in book}}
